ddls:
CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device (e.g., sensor, actuator, controller) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the device */,
  "device_model" TEXT /* Model of the device */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the device */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Installation date of the device */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensors" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device the sensor belongs to */,
  "sensor_type" TEXT /* Type of sensor (e.g., temperature, pressure, flow rate) */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the sensor reading */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sensor */,
  "min_value" REAL /* Minimum value of the sensor reading */,
  "max_value" REAL /* Maximum value of the sensor reading */,
  "accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the sensor reading */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensors_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "actuators" (
  "actuator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each actuator */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device the actuator belongs to */,
  "actuator_type" TEXT /* Type of actuator (e.g., pump, valve, motor) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the actuator */,
  "min_output" REAL /* Minimum output of the actuator */,
  "max_output" REAL /* Maximum output of the actuator */,
  "resolution" REAL /* Resolution of the actuator output */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("actuator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_actuators_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "control_logic" (
  "logic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each control logic */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device the control logic belongs to */,
  "parameter" TEXT /* Parameter name (e.g., setpoint, gain, bias) */,
  "value" TEXT /* Parameter value */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the control logic */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("logic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_control_logic_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "parameters" (
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each parameter */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device the parameter belongs to */,
  "parameter_name" TEXT /* Name of the parameter (e.g., REQ, MODE, MESGN) */,
  "parameter_value" TEXT /* Value of the parameter */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the parameter */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("parameter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_parameters_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "control_modes" (
  "mode_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each control mode */,
  "mode_name" TEXT /* Name of the control mode (e.g., Manual, Automatic, Remote) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the control mode */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mode_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the data type (e.g., BOOL, BYTE, DWORD) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "memory_areas" (
  "area_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each memory area */,
  "area_name" TEXT /* Name of the memory area (e.g., I, Q, M, D, L, constant) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the memory area */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("area_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "messages" (
  "message_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each message */,
  "message_name" TEXT /* Name of the message (e.g., MESGN) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the message */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("message_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "errors" (
  "error_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each error */,
  "error_code" INTEGER /* Error code */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Error message */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the error */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("error_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the log entry */,
  "event" TEXT /* Event type (e.g., alarm, warning, info) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who triggered the log entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role (e.g., admin, operator, engineer) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alarms" (
  "alarm_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alarm */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device the alarm belongs to */,
  "alarm_type" TEXT /* Type of alarm (e.g., high, low, fault) */,
  "threshold" REAL /* Threshold value for the alarm */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alarm */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alarm_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alarms_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system setting */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the system setting */,
  "setting_value" TEXT /* Value of the system setting */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the system setting */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_calibration" (
  "calibration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device calibration */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device the calibration belongs to */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date of the calibration */,
  "calibration_value" REAL /* Calibration value */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("calibration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_calibration_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system event */,
  "event_name" TEXT /* Name of the system event */,
  "event_description" TEXT /* Description of the system event */,
  "event_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the system event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id")
)
